title: Vecs 1
description: Vectors are resizable arrays. You can create them with `Vec::new()` or the `vec!` macro.
instructions: Create a vector that passes the tests using either Vec::new() with push, or the vec! macro.
hints: You can use the `vec!` macro to create a vector with initial values.
explanation: Vectors (`Vec<T>`) are dynamically-sized arrays allocated on the heap. The `vec!` macro provides convenient syntax for creating vectors with initial values, similar to array literal syntax. You can also create an empty vector with `Vec::new()` and add elements with `.push()`.
